title: Voluntary Predation
definition: A unique class of predator-prey interaction, characterized by voluntary provisioning within an involuntary relationship.

difficulty_levels.beginner: In this relationship, the prey (host) offers food willingly to a predator. It's like a cat letting you pet it — it doesn't want to be petted, but it tolerates it.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: This interaction goes beyond typical predator-prey relationships by acknowledging the inevitable feeding arrangement and setting terms through generosity rather than resistance.
difficulty_levels.expert: The offering ritual modifies asymmetric biological relationships by demonstrating acceptance, resource sufficiency, and psychological stability. The predator responds with reduced feeding frequency or intensity, creating a stable relationship that structurally parallels peace-making rituals in human conflict.

extra_fields.insight: Ritual can modify the dynamics of even the most asymmetric biological relationships, transforming predation into an iterated negotiation.
